NKJV.1: Better is a dry morsel with quietness, Than a house full of feasting with strife.
NKJV.2: A wise servant will rule over a son who causes shame, And will share an inheritance among the brothers.
NKJV.3: The refining pot is for silver and the furnace for gold, But the LORD tests the hearts.
NKJV.4: An evildoer gives heed to false lips; A liar listens eagerly to a spiteful tongue.
NKJV.5: He who mocks the poor reproaches his Maker;  He who is glad at calamity will not go unpunished.
NKJV.6: Children’s children are the crown of old men, And the glory of children is their father.
NKJV.7: Excellent speech is not becoming to a fool, Much less lying lips to a prince.
NKJV.8: A present is a precious stone in the eyes of its possessor; Wherever he turns, he prospers.
NKJV.9: He who covers a transgression seeks love, But he who repeats a matter separates friends.
NKJV.10: Rebuke is more effective for a wise man Than a hundred blows on a fool.
NKJV.11: An evil man seeks only rebellion; Therefore a cruel messenger will be sent against him.
NKJV.12: Let a man meet a bear robbed of her cubs, Rather than a fool in his folly.
NKJV.13: Whoever rewards evil for good, Evil will not depart from his house.
NKJV.14: The beginning of strife is like releasing water; Therefore stop contention before a quarrel starts.
NKJV.15: He who justifies the wicked, and he who condemns the just, Both of them alike are an abomination to the LORD.
NKJV.16: Why is there in the hand of a fool the purchase price of wisdom, Since he has no heart for it?
NKJV.17: A friend loves at all times, And a brother is born for adversity.
NKJV.18: A man devoid of understanding shakes hands in a pledge, And becomes surety for his friend.
NKJV.19: He who loves transgression loves strife, And he who exalts his gate seeks destruction.
NKJV.20: He who has a deceitful heart finds no good, And he who has a perverse tongue falls into evil.
NKJV.21: He who begets a scoffer does so to his sorrow, And the father of a fool has no joy.
NKJV.22: A merry heart does good, like medicine, But a broken spirit dries the bones.
NKJV.23: A wicked man accepts a bribe behind the back To pervert the ways of justice.
NKJV.24: Wisdom is in the sight of him who has understanding, But the eyes of a fool are on the ends of the earth.
NKJV.25: A foolish son is a grief to his father, And bitterness to her who bore him.
NKJV.26: Also, to punish the righteous is not good, Nor to strike princes for their uprightness.
NKJV.27: He who has knowledge spares his words, And a man of understanding is of a calm spirit.
NKJV.28: Even a fool is counted wise when he holds his peace; When he shuts his lips, he is considered perceptive.

ESV.1: Better is a dry morsel with quiet than a house full of feasting  with strife.
ESV.2: A servant who deals wisely will rule over a son who acts shamefully and will share the inheritance as one of the brothers.
ESV.3: The crucible is for silver, and the furnace is for gold, and the LORD tests hearts.
ESV.4: An evildoer listens to wicked lips, and a liar gives ear to a mischievous tongue.
ESV.5: Whoever mocks the poor insults his Maker; he who is glad at calamity will not go unpunished.
ESV.6: Grandchildren are the crown of the aged, and the glory of children is their fathers.
ESV.7: Fine speech is not becoming to a fool; still less is false speech to a prince.
ESV.8: A bribe is like a magic  stone in the eyes of the one who gives it; wherever he turns he prospers.
ESV.9: Whoever covers an offense seeks love, but he who repeats a matter separates close friends.
ESV.10: A rebuke goes deeper into a man of understanding than a hundred blows into a fool.
ESV.11: An evil man seeks only rebellion, and a cruel messenger will be sent against him.
ESV.12: Let a man meet a she-bear robbed of her cubs rather than a fool in his folly.
ESV.13: If anyone returns evil for good, evil will not depart from his house.
ESV.14: The beginning of strife is like letting out water, so quit before the quarrel breaks out.
ESV.15: He who justifies the wicked and he who condemns the righteous are both alike an abomination to the LORD.
ESV.16: Why should a fool have money in his hand to buy wisdom when he has no sense?
ESV.17: A friend loves at all times, and a brother is born for adversity.
ESV.18: One who lacks sense gives a pledge and puts up security in the presence of his neighbor.
ESV.19: Whoever loves transgression loves strife; he who makes his door high seeks destruction.
ESV.20: A man of crooked heart does not discover good, and one with a dishonest tongue falls into calamity.
ESV.21: He who sires a fool gets himself sorrow, and the father of a fool has no joy.
ESV.22: A joyful heart is good medicine, but a crushed spirit dries up the bones.
ESV.23: The wicked accepts a bribe in secret  to pervert the ways of justice.
ESV.24: The discerning sets his face toward wisdom, but the eyes of a fool are on the ends of the earth.
ESV.25: A foolish son is a grief to his father and bitterness to her who bore him.
ESV.26: To impose a fine on a righteous man is not good, nor to strike the noble for their uprightness.
ESV.27: Whoever restrains his words has knowledge, and he who has a cool spirit is a man of understanding.
ESV.28: Even a fool who keeps silent is considered wise; when he closes his lips, he is deemed intelligent.

NASB.1: Better is a dry morsel and quietness with it Than a house full of feasting with strife.
NASB.2: A servant who acts wisely will rule over a son who acts shamefully, And will share in the inheritance among brothers.
NASB.3: The refining pot is for silver and the furnace for gold, But the LORD tests hearts.
NASB.4: An evildoer listens to wicked lips; A liar pays attention to a destructive tongue.
NASB.5: He who mocks the poor taunts his Maker; He who rejoices at calamity will not go unpunished.
NASB.6: Grandchildren are the crown of old men, And the glory of sons is their fathers.
NASB.7: Excellent speech is not fitting for a fool, Much less are lying lips to a prince.
NASB.8: A bribe is a charm in the sight of its owner; Wherever he turns, he prospers.
NASB.9: He who conceals a transgression seeks love, But he who repeats a matter separates intimate friends.
NASB.10: A rebuke goes deeper into one who has understanding Than a hundred blows into a fool.
NASB.11: A rebellious man seeks only evil, So a cruel messenger will be sent against him.
NASB.12: Let a man meet a bear robbed of her cubs, Rather than a fool in his folly.
NASB.13: He who returns evil for good, Evil will not depart from his house.
NASB.14: The beginning of strife is [like] letting out water, So abandon the quarrel before it breaks out.
NASB.15: He who justifies the wicked and he who condemns the righteous, Both of them alike are an abomination to the LORD.
NASB.16: Why is there a price in the hand of a fool to buy wisdom, When he has no sense?
NASB.17: A friend loves at all times, And a brother is born for adversity.
NASB.18: A man lacking in sense pledges And becomes guarantor in the presence of his neighbor.
NASB.19: He who loves transgression loves strife; He who raises his door seeks destruction.
NASB.20: He who has a crooked mind finds no good, And he who is perverted in his language falls into evil.
NASB.21: He who sires a fool [does so] to his sorrow, And the father of a fool has no joy.
NASB.22: A joyful heart is good medicine, But a broken spirit dries up the bones.
NASB.23: A wicked man receives a bribe from the bosom To pervert the ways of justice.
NASB.24: Wisdom is in the presence of the one who has understanding, But the eyes of a fool are on the ends of the earth.
NASB.25: A foolish son is a grief to his father And bitterness to her who bore him.
NASB.26: It is also not good to fine the righteous, [Nor] to strike the noble for [their] uprightness.
NASB.27: He who restrains his words has knowledge, And he who has a cool spirit is a man of understanding.
NASB.28: Even a fool, when he keeps silent, is considered wise; When he closes his lips, he is [considered] prudent.

NLT.1:
Better a dry crust eaten in peace
than a house filled with feasting — and conflict.
NLT.2:
A wise servant will rule over the master’s disgraceful son
and will share the inheritance of the master’s children.
NLT.3:
Fire tests the purity of silver and gold,
but the LORD tests the heart.
NLT.4:
Wrongdoers eagerly listen to gossip;
liars pay close attention to slander.
NLT.5:
Those who mock the poor insult their Maker;
those who rejoice at the misfortune of others will be punished.
NLT.6:
Grandchildren are the crowning glory of the aged;
parents are the pride of their children.
NLT.7:
Eloquent words are not fitting for a fool;
even less are lies fitting for a ruler.
NLT.8:
A bribe is like a lucky charm;
whoever gives one will prosper!
NLT.9:
Love prospers when a fault is forgiven,
but dwelling on it separates close friends.
NLT.10:
A single rebuke does more for a person of understanding
than a hundred lashes on the back of a fool.
NLT.11:
Evil people are eager for rebellion,
but they will be severely punished.
NLT.12:
It is safer to meet a bear robbed of her cubs
than to confront a fool caught in foolishness.
NLT.13:
If you repay good with evil,
evil will never leave your house.
NLT.14:
Starting a quarrel is like opening a floodgate,
so stop before a dispute breaks out.
NLT.15:
Acquitting the guilty and condemning the innocent —
both are detestable to the LORD.
NLT.16:
It is senseless to pay to educate a fool,
since he has no heart for learning.
NLT.17:
A friend is always loyal,
and a brother is born to help in time of need.
NLT.18:
It’s poor judgment to guarantee another person’s debt
or put up security for a friend.
NLT.19:
Anyone who loves to quarrel loves sin;
anyone who trusts in high walls invites disaster.
NLT.20:
The crooked heart will not prosper;
the lying tongue tumbles into trouble.
NLT.21:
It is painful to be the parent of a fool;
there is no joy for the father of a rebel.
NLT.22:
A cheerful heart is good medicine,
but a broken spirit saps a person’s strength.
NLT.23:
The wicked take secret bribes
to pervert the course of justice.
NLT.24:
Sensible people keep their eyes glued on wisdom,
but a fool’s eyes wander to the ends of the earth.
NLT.25:
Foolish children bring grief to their father
and bitterness to the one who gave them birth.
NLT.26:
It is wrong to punish the godly for being good
or to flog leaders for being honest.
NLT.27:
A truly wise person uses few words;
a person with understanding is even-tempered.
NLT.28:
Even fools are thought wise when they keep silent;
with their mouths shut, they seem intelligent.

WEB.1: Better is a dry morsel with quietness, than a house full of feasting with strife.
WEB.2: A servant who deals wisely will rule over a son who causes shame, and shall have a part in the inheritance among the brothers.
WEB.3: The refining pot is for silver, and the furnace for gold, but Yahweh tests the hearts.
WEB.4: An evildoer heeds wicked lips. A liar gives ear to a mischievous tongue.
WEB.5: Whoever mocks the poor reproaches his Maker. He who is glad at calamity shall not be unpunished.
WEB.6: Children’s children are the crown of old men; the glory of children are their parents.
WEB.7: Arrogant speech isn’t fitting for a fool, much less do lying lips fit a prince.
WEB.8: A bribe is a precious stone in the eyes of him who gives it; wherever he turns, he prospers.
WEB.9: He who covers an offense promotes love; but he who repeats a matter separates best friends.
WEB.10: A rebuke enters deeper into one who has understanding than a hundred lashes into a fool.
WEB.11: An evil man seeks only rebellion; therefore a cruel messenger shall be sent against him.
WEB.12: Let a bear robbed of her cubs meet a man, rather than a fool in his folly.
WEB.13: Whoever rewards evil for good, evil shall not depart from his house.
WEB.14: The beginning of strife is like breaching a dam, therefore stop contention before quarreling breaks out.
WEB.15: He who justifies the wicked, and he who condemns the righteous, both of them alike are an abomination to Yahweh.
WEB.16: Why is there money in the hand of a fool to buy wisdom, since he has no understanding?
WEB.17: A friend loves at all times; and a brother is born for adversity.
WEB.18: A man void of understanding strikes hands, and becomes collateral in the presence of his neighbor.
WEB.19: He who loves disobedience loves strife. One who builds a high gate seeks destruction.
WEB.20: One who has a perverse heart doesn’t find prosperity, and one who has a deceitful tongue falls into trouble.
WEB.21: He who becomes the father of a fool grieves. The father of a fool has no joy.
WEB.22: A cheerful heart makes good medicine, but a crushed spirit dries up the bones.
WEB.23: A wicked man receives a bribe in secret, to pervert the ways of justice.
WEB.24: Wisdom is before the face of one who has understanding, but the eyes of a fool wander to the ends of the earth.
WEB.25: A foolish son brings grief to his father, and bitterness to her who bore him.
WEB.26: Also to punish the righteous is not good, nor to flog officials for their integrity.
WEB.27: He who spares his words has knowledge. He who is even tempered is a man of understanding.
WEB.28: Even a fool, when he keeps silent, is counted wise. When he shuts his lips, he is thought to be discerning.

CSB17.1:
Better a dry crust with peace
than a house full of feasting with strife. ⓔ
CSB17.2:
A prudent servant will rule over a disgraceful son
and share an inheritance among brothers.
CSB17.3:
A crucible for silver, and a smelter for gold, ⓕ
and the LORD is the tester of hearts. ⓖ
CSB17.4:
A wicked person listens to malicious talk; [1]
a liar pays attention to a destructive tongue.
CSB17.5:
The one who mocks the poor insults his Maker, ⓗ
and one who rejoices over calamity
will not go unpunished. ⓘ
CSB17.6:
Grandchildren are the crown of the elderly,
and the pride of children is their fathers.
CSB17.7:
Eloquent words are not appropriate on a fool’s lips;
how much worse are lies for a ruler.
CSB17.8:
A bribe ⓙ seems like a magic stone to its owner;
wherever he turns, he succeeds. ⓚ
CSB17.9:
Whoever conceals an offense promotes love, ⓛ
but whoever gossips about it separates friends. ⓜ
CSB17.10:
A rebuke cuts into a perceptive person
more than a hundred lashes into a fool.
CSB17.11:
An evil person desires only rebellion;
a cruel messenger [2] ⓝ will be sent against him.
CSB17.12:
Better for a person to meet a bear robbed of her cubs ⓐ
than a fool in his foolishness.
CSB17.13:
If anyone returns evil for good, ⓑ
evil will never depart from his house. ⓒ
CSB17.14:
To start a conflict is to release a flood;
stop the dispute before it breaks out. ⓓ
CSB17.15:
Acquitting the guilty and condemning the just ⓔ  —
both are detestable to the LORD.
CSB17.16:
Why does a fool have money in his hand
with no intention of buying wisdom? ⓕ
CSB17.17:
A friend loves at all times, ⓖ
and a brother is born for a difficult time.
CSB17.18:
One without sense enters an agreement [3]
and puts up security for his friend. ⓗ
CSB17.19:
One who loves to offend loves strife; ⓘ
one who builds a high threshold invites injury.
CSB17.20:
One with a twisted mind will not succeed,
and one with deceitful speech will fall into ruin. ⓙ
CSB17.21:
A man fathers a fool to his own sorrow;
the father of a fool has no joy. ⓚ
CSB17.22:
A joyful heart is good medicine,
but a broken spirit dries up the bones. ⓛ
CSB17.23:
A wicked person secretly takes a bribe ⓜ
to subvert the course of justice. ⓝ
CSB17.24:
Wisdom is the focus of the perceptive,
but a fool’s eyes ⓐ roam to the ends of the earth.
CSB17.25:
A foolish son is grief to his father
and bitterness to the one who bore him. ⓑ
CSB17.26:
It is certainly not good to fine an innocent person
or to beat a noble for his honesty. [4] ⓒ
CSB17.27:
The one who has knowledge restrains his words, ⓓ
and one who keeps a cool head [5]
is a person of understanding.
CSB17.28:
Even a fool is considered wise when he keeps silent —
discerning, when he seals his lips. ⓔ

AMP.1:
Better is a dry morsel [of food served] with quietness and peace
Than a house full of feasting [served] with strife and contention.
AMP.2:
A wise servant will rule over the [unworthy] son who acts shamefully and brings disgrace [to the family]
And [the worthy servant] will share in the inheritance among the brothers.
AMP.3:
The refining pot is for silver and the furnace for gold,
But the LORD tests hearts.
AMP.4:
An evildoer listens closely to wicked lips;
And a liar pays attention to a destructive and malicious tongue.
AMP.5:
Whoever mocks the poor taunts his Maker,
And he who rejoices at [another's] disaster will not go unpunished.
AMP.6:
Grandchildren are the crown of aged men,
And the glory of children is their fathers [who live godly lives].
AMP.7:
Excellent speech does not benefit a fool [who is spiritually blind],
Much less do lying lips benefit a prince.
AMP.8:
A bribe is like a bright, precious stone in the eyes of its owner;
Wherever he turns, he prospers.
AMP.9:
He who covers and forgives an offense seeks love,
But he who repeats or gossips about a matter separates intimate friends.
AMP.10:
A reprimand goes deeper into one who has understanding and a teachable spirit
Than a hundred lashes into a fool.
AMP.11:
A rebellious man seeks only evil;
Therefore a cruel messenger will be sent against him.
AMP.12:
Let a man meet a [ferocious] bear robbed of her cubs
Rather than the [angry, narcissistic] fool in his folly.
AMP.13:
Whoever returns evil for good,
Evil will not depart from his house.
AMP.14:
The beginning of strife is like letting out water [as from a small break in a dam; first it trickles and then it gushes];
Therefore abandon the quarrel before it breaks out and tempers explode.
AMP.15:
He who justifies the wicked, and he who condemns the righteous
Are both repulsive to the LORD.
AMP.16:
Why is there money in the hand of a fool to buy wisdom,
When he has no common sense or even a heart for it?
AMP.17:
A friend loves at all times,
And a brother is born for adversity.
AMP.18:
A man lacking common sense gives a pledge
And becomes guarantor [for the debt of another] in the presence of his neighbor.
AMP.19:
He who loves transgression loves strife and is quarrelsome;
He who [proudly] raises his gate seeks destruction [because of his arrogant pride].
AMP.20:
He who has a crooked mind finds no good,
And he who is perverted in his language falls into evil.
AMP.21:
He who becomes the parent of a fool [who is spiritually blind] does so to his sorrow,
And the father of a fool [who is spiritually blind] has no joy.
AMP.22:
A happy heart is good medicine and a joyful mind causes healing,
But a broken spirit dries up the bones.
AMP.23:
A wicked man receives a bribe from the [hidden] pocket
To pervert the ways of justice.
AMP.24:
[Skillful and godly] wisdom is in the presence of a person of understanding [and he recognizes it],
But the eyes of a [thickheaded] fool are on the ends of the earth.
AMP.25:
A foolish son is a grief and anguish to his father
And bitterness to her who gave birth to him.
AMP.26:
It is also not good to fine the righteous,
Nor to strike the noble for their uprightness.
AMP.27:
He who has knowledge restrains and is careful with his words,
And a man of understanding and wisdom has a cool spirit (self-control, an even temper).
AMP.28:
Even a [callous, arrogant] fool, when he keeps silent, is considered wise;
When he closes his lips he is regarded as sensible (prudent, discreet) and a man of understanding.

MSG.1:
A meal of bread and water in contented peace
is better than a banquet spiced with quarrels.
MSG.2:
A wise servant takes charge of an unruly child
and is honored as one of the family.
MSG.3:
As silver in a crucible and gold in a pan,
so our lives are assayed by God.
MSG.4:
Evil people relish malicious conversation;
the ears of liars itch for dirty gossip.
MSG.5:
Whoever mocks poor people, insults their Creator;
gloating over misfortune is a punishable crime.
MSG.6:
Old people are distinguished by grandchildren;
children take pride in their parents.
MSG.7:
We don't expect eloquence from fools,
nor do we expect lies from our leaders.
MSG.8:
Receiving a gift is like getting a rare gemstone;
any way you look at it, you see beauty refracted.
MSG.9:
Overlook an offense and bond a friendship;
fasten on to a slight and—good-bye, friend!
MSG.10:
A quiet rebuke to a person of good sense
does more than a whack on the head of a fool.
MSG.11:
Criminals out looking for nothing but trouble
won't have to wait long—they'll meet it coming and going!
MSG.12:
Better to meet a grizzly robbed of her cubs
than a fool hellbent on folly.
MSG.13:
Those who return evil for good
will meet their own evil returning.
MSG.14:
The start of a quarrel is like a leak in a dam,
so stop it before it bursts.
MSG.15:
Whitewashing bad people and throwing mud on good people
are equally abhorrent to God.
MSG.16:
What's this? Fools out shopping for wisdom!
They wouldn't recognize it if they saw it!
MSG.17:
Friends love through all kinds of weather,
and families stick together in all kinds of trouble.
MSG.18:
It's stupid to try to get something for nothing,
or run up huge bills you can never pay.
MSG.19:
The person who courts sin, marries trouble;
build a wall, invite a burglar.
MSG.20:
A bad motive can't achieve a good end;
double-talk brings you double trouble.
MSG.21:
Having a fool for a child is misery;
it's no fun being the parent of a dolt.
MSG.22:
A cheerful disposition is good for your health;
gloom and doom leave you bone-tired.
MSG.23:
The wicked take bribes under the table;
they show nothing but contempt for justice.
MSG.24:
The perceptive find wisdom in their own front yard;
fools look for it everywhere but right here.
MSG.25:
A surly, stupid child is sheer pain to a father,
a bitter pill for a mother to swallow.
MSG.26:
It's wrong to penalize good behavior,
or make good citizens pay for the crimes of others.
MSG.27:
The one who knows much says little;
an understanding person remains calm.
MSG.28:
Even dunces who keep quiet are thought to be wise;
as long as they keep their mouths shut, they're smart.

NIV.1:
Better a dry crust with peace and quiet
than a house full of feasting, with strife.
NIV.2:
A wise servant will rule over a disgraceful son,
and will share the inheritance as one of the brothers.
NIV.3:
The crucible for silver and the furnace for gold,
but the Lord tests the heart.
NIV.4:
A wicked man listens to evil lips;
a liar pays attention to a malicious tongue.
NIV.5:
He who mocks the poor shows contempt for their Maker;
whoever gloats over disaster will not go unpunished.
NIV.6:
Children’s children are a crown to the aged,
and parents are the pride of their children.
NIV.7:
Arrogant lips are unsuited to a fool—
how much worse lying lips to a ruler!
NIV.8:
A bribe is a charm to the one who gives it;
wherever he turns, he succeeds.
NIV.9:
He who covers over an offense promotes love,
but whoever repeats the matter separates close friends.
NIV.10:
A rebuke impresses a man of discernment
more than a hundred lashes a fool.
NIV.11:
An evil man is bent only on rebellion;
a merciless official will be sent against him.
NIV.12:
Better to meet a bear robbed of her cubs
than a fool in his folly.
NIV.13:
If a man pays back evil for good,
evil will never leave his house.
NIV.14:
Starting a quarrel is like breaching a dam;
so drop the matter before a dispute breaks out.
NIV.15:
Acquitting the guilty and condemning the innocent—
the Lord detests them both.
NIV.16:
Of what use is money in the hand of a fool,
since he has no desire to get wisdom?
NIV.17:
A friend loves at all times,
and a brother is born for adversity.
NIV.18:
A man lacking in judgment strikes hands in pledge
and puts up security for his neighbor.
NIV.19:
He who loves a quarrel loves sin;
he who builds a high gate invites destruction.
NIV.20:
A man of perverse heart does not prosper;
he whose tongue is deceitful falls into trouble.
NIV.21:
To have a fool for a son brings grief;
there is no joy for the father of a fool.
NIV.22:
A cheerful heart is good medicine,
but a crushed spirit dries up the bones.
NIV.23:
A wicked man accepts a bribe in secret
to pervert the course of justice.
NIV.24:
A discerning man keeps wisdom in view,
but a fool’s eyes wander to the ends of the earth.
NIV.25:
A foolish son brings grief to his father
and bitterness to the one who bore him.
NIV.26:
It is not good to punish an innocent man,
or to flog officials for their integrity.
NIV.27:
A man of knowledge uses words with restraint,
and a man of understanding is even-tempered.
NIV.28:
Even a fool is thought wise if he keeps silent,
and discerning if he holds his tongue.

NRSVCE.1:
Better is a dry morsel with quiet
than a house full of feasting with strife.
NRSVCE.2:
A slave who deals wisely will rule over a child who acts shamefully,
and will share the inheritance as one of the family.
NRSVCE.3:
The crucible is for silver, and the furnace is for gold,
but the LORD tests the heart.
NRSVCE.4:
An evildoer listens to wicked lips;
and a liar gives heed to a mischievous tongue.
NRSVCE.5:
Those who mock the poor insult their Maker;
those who are glad at calamity will not go unpunished.
NRSVCE.6:
Grandchildren are the crown of the aged,
and the glory of children is their parents.
NRSVCE.7:
Fine speech is not becoming to a fool;
still less is false speech to a ruler.
NRSVCE.8:
A bribe is like a magic stone in the eyes of those who give it;
wherever they turn they prosper.
NRSVCE.9:
One who forgives an affront fosters friendship,
but one who dwells on disputes will alienate a friend.
NRSVCE.10:
A rebuke strikes deeper into a discerning person
than a hundred blows into a fool.
NRSVCE.11:
Evil people seek only rebellion,
but a cruel messenger will be sent against them.
NRSVCE.12:
Better to meet a she-bear robbed of its cubs
than to confront a fool immersed in folly.
NRSVCE.13:
Evil will not depart from the house
of one who returns evil for good.
NRSVCE.14:
The beginning of strife is like letting out water;
so stop before the quarrel breaks out.
NRSVCE.15:
One who justifies the wicked and one who condemns the righteous
are both alike an abomination to the LORD.
NRSVCE.16:
Why should fools have a price in hand
to buy wisdom, when they have no mind to learn?
NRSVCE.17:
A friend loves at all times,
and kinsfolk are born to share adversity.
NRSVCE.18:
It is senseless to give a pledge,
to become surety for a neighbor.
NRSVCE.19:
One who loves transgression loves strife;
one who builds a high threshold invites broken bones.
NRSVCE.20:
The crooked of mind do not prosper,
and the perverse of tongue fall into calamity.
NRSVCE.21:
The one who begets a fool gets trouble;
the parent of a fool has no joy.
NRSVCE.22:
A cheerful heart is a good medicine,
but a downcast spirit dries up the bones.
NRSVCE.23:
The wicked accept a concealed bribe
to pervert the ways of justice.
NRSVCE.24:
The discerning person looks to wisdom,
but the eyes of a fool to the ends of the earth.
NRSVCE.25:
Foolish children are a grief to their father
and bitterness to her who bore them.
NRSVCE.26:
To impose a fine on the innocent is not right,
or to flog the noble for their integrity.
NRSVCE.27:
One who spares words is knowledgeable;
one who is cool in spirit has understanding.
NRSVCE.28:
Even fools who keep silent are considered wise;
when they close their lips, they are deemed intelligent.

NET.1: Better is a dry crust of bread where there is quietness than a house full of feasting with strife.
NET.2: A servant who acts wisely will rule over an heir who behaves shamefully, and will share the inheritance along with the relatives.
NET.3: The crucible is for refining silver and the furnace is for gold, likewise the LORD tests hearts.
NET.4: One who acts wickedly pays attention to evil counsel; a liar listens to a malicious tongue.
NET.5: The one who mocks the poor insults his Creator; whoever rejoices over disaster will not go unpunished.
NET.6: Grandchildren are like a crown to the elderly, and the glory of children is their parents.
NET.7: Excessive speech is not becoming for a fool; how much less are lies for a ruler!
NET.8: A bribe works like a charm for the one who offers it; in whatever he does he succeeds.
NET.9: The one who forgives an offense seeks love, but whoever repeats a matter separates close friends.
NET.10: A rebuke makes a greater impression on a discerning person than a hundred blows on a fool.
NET.11: An evil person seeks only rebellion, and so a cruel messenger will be sent against him.
NET.12: It is better for a person to meet a mother bear being robbed of her cubs, than to encounter a fool in his folly.
NET.13: As for the one who repays evil for good, evil will not leave his house.
NET.14: Starting a quarrel is like letting out water; stop it before strife breaks out!
NET.15: The one who acquits the guilty and the one who condemns the innocent — both of them are an abomination to the LORD.
NET.16: Of what use is money in the hand of a fool, since he has no intention of acquiring wisdom?
NET.17: A friend loves at all times, and a relative is born to help in adversity.
NET.18: The one who lacks wisdom strikes hands in pledge, and puts up financial security for his neighbor.
NET.19: The one who loves a quarrel loves transgression; whoever builds his gate high seeks destruction.
NET.20: The one who has a perverse heart does not find good, and the one who is deceitful in speech falls into trouble.
NET.21: Whoever brings a fool into the world does so to his grief, and the father of a fool has no joy.
NET.22: A cheerful heart brings good healing, but a crushed spirit dries up the bones.
NET.23: A wicked person receives a bribe secretly to pervert the ways of justice.
NET.24: Wisdom is directly in front of the discerning person, but the eyes of a fool run to the ends of the earth.
NET.25: A foolish child is a grief to his father, and bitterness to the mother who bore him.
NET.26: It is terrible to punish a righteous person, and to flog honorable men is wrong.
NET.27: The truly wise person restrains his words, and the one who stays calm is discerning.
NET.28: Even a fool who remains silent is considered wise, and the one who holds his tongue is deemed discerning.
